heading: Procedures for initiating an antidumping duty investigation
label: 1673a Procedures for initiating an antidumping duty investigation

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An antidumping duty investigation shall be initiated whenever the administering authority determines, from information available to it, that a formal investigation is warranted into the question of whether the elements necessary for the imposition of a duty under exist.section 1673 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: more than one antidumping order is in effect with respect to that class or kind of merchandise;

children.label: ii
children.text: in the judgment of the administering authority there is reason to believe or suspect an extraordinary pattern of persistent injurious dumping from one or more additional supplier countries; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the judgment of the administering authority this extraordinary pattern is causing a serious commercial problem for the domestic industry.

children.heading: Initiation of investigation
children.label: B Initiation of investigation
children.text: If during the period of monitoring referred to in subparagraph (A), the administering authority determines that there is sufficient information to initiate a formal investigation under this subsection regarding an additional supplier country, the administering authority shall immediately initiate such an investigation.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: C Definition
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “additional supplier country” means a country regarding which no antidumping investigation is currently pending, and no antidumping duty order is currently in effect, with respect to imports of the class or kind of merchandise covered by subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Expeditious action
children.label: D Expeditious action
children.text: The administering authority and the Commission, to the extent practicable, shall expedite proceedings under this part undertaken as a result of a formal investigation initiated under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Petition requirements
children.label: 1 Petition requirements
children.text: An antidumping proceeding shall be initiated whenever an interested party described in subparagraph (C), (D), (E), (F), or (G) of files a petition with the administering authority, on behalf of an industry, which alleges the elements necessary for the imposition of the duty imposed by , and which is accompanied by information reasonably available to the petitioner supporting those allegations. The petition may be amended at such time, and upon such conditions, as the administering authority and the Commission may permit.section 1677(9) of this titlesection 1673 of this title

children.heading: Simultaneous filing with Commission
children.label: 2 Simultaneous filing with Commission
children.text: The petitioner shall file a copy of the petition with the Commission on the same day as it is filed with the administering authority.

children.heading: Notification of governments
children.label: A Notification of governments
children.text: Upon receipt of a petition filed under paragraph (1), the administering authority shall notify the government of any exporting country named in the petition by delivering a public version of the petition to an appropriate representative of such country.

children.heading: Acceptance of communications
children.label: B Acceptance of communications
children.text: The administering authority shall not accept any unsolicited oral or written communication from any person other than an interested party described in section 1677(9)(C), (D), (E), (F), or (G) of this title before the administering authority makes its decision whether to initiate an investigation, except as provided in subsection (c)(4)(D), and except for inquiries regarding the status of the administering authority’s consideration of the petition.

children.heading: Nondisclosure of certain information
children.label: C Nondisclosure of certain information
children.text: The administering authority and the Commission shall not disclose information with regard to any draft petition submitted for review and comment before it is filed under paragraph (1).

children.label: i
children.text: after examining, on the basis of sources readily available to the administering authority, the accuracy and adequacy of the evidence provided in the petition, determine whether the petition alleges the elements necessary for the imposition of a duty under and contains information reasonably available to the petitioner supporting the allegations, andsection 1673 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: determine if the petition has been filed by or on behalf of the industry.

children.heading: Extension of time
children.label: B Extension of time
children.text: In any case in which the administering authority is required to poll or otherwise determine support for the petition by the industry under paragraph (4)(D), the administering authority may, in exceptional circumstances, apply subparagraph (A) by substituting “a maximum of 40 days” for “20 days”.

children.heading: Time limits where petition involves same merchandise as an order that has been revoked
children.label: C Time limits where petition involves same merchandise as an order that has been revoked
i
children.text: an antidumping duty order or finding that was revoked under in the 24 months preceding the date the petition is filed, orsection 1675(d) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a suspended investigation that was terminated under in the 24 months preceding the date the petition is filed,section 1675(d) of this title

children.heading: Affirmative determinations
children.label: 2 Affirmative determinations
children.text: If the determinations under clauses (i) and (ii) of paragraph (1)(A) are affirmative, the administering authority shall initiate an investigation to determine whether the subject merchandise is being, or is likely to be, sold in the United States at less than its fair value.

children.heading: Negative determinations
children.label: 3 Negative determinations
children.text: If the determination under clause (i) or (ii) of paragraph (1)(A) is negative, the administering authority shall dismiss the petition, terminate the proceeding, and notify the petitioner in writing of the reasons for the determination.

children.label: i
children.text: the domestic producers or workers who support the petition account for at least 25 percent of the total production of the domestic like product, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the domestic producers or workers who support the petition account for more than 50 percent of the production of the domestic like product produced by that portion of the industry expressing support for or opposition to the petition.

children.heading: Producers related to foreign producers
children.label: i Producers related to foreign producers
children.text: In determining industry support under subparagraph (A), the administering authority shall disregard the position of domestic producers who oppose the petition, if such producers are related to foreign producers, as defined in , unless such domestic producers demonstrate that their interests as domestic producers would be adversely affected by the imposition of an antidumping duty order.section 1677(4)(B)(ii) of this title

children.heading: Producers who are importers
children.label: ii Producers who are importers
children.text: The administering authority may disregard the position of domestic producers of a domestic like product who are importers of the subject merchandise.

children.heading: Special rule for regional industries
children.label: C Special rule for regional industries
children.text: If the petition alleges the industry is a regional industry, the administering authority shall determine whether the petition has been filed by or on behalf of the industry by applying subparagraph (A) on the basis of production in the region.

children.label: i
children.text: poll the industry or rely on other information in order to determine if there is support for the petition as required by subparagraph (A), or

children.label: ii
children.text: if there is a large number of producers in the industry, the administering authority may determine industry support for the petition by using any statistically valid sampling method to poll the industry.

children.heading: Comments by interested parties
children.label: E Comments by interested parties
children.text: Before the administering authority makes a determination with respect to initiating an investigation, any person who would qualify as an interested party under if an investigation were initiated, may submit comments or information on the issue of industry support. After the administering authority makes a determination with respect to initiating an investigation, the determination regarding industry support shall not be reconsidered.section 1677(9) of this title

children.heading: “Domestic producers or workers” defined
children.label: 5 “Domestic producers or workers” defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “domestic producers or workers” means those interested parties who are eligible to file a petition under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Notification to Commission of determination
children.label: d Notification to Commission of determination

children.text: notify the Commission immediately of any determination it makes under subsection (a) or (c), and

if the determination is affirmative, make available to the Commission such information as it may have relating to the matter under investigation, under such procedures as the administering authority and the Commission may establish to prevent disclosure, other than with the consent of the party providing it or under protective order, of any information to which confidential treatment has been given by the administering authority.
there is a history of dumping in the United States or elsewhere of the subject merchandise, or
the person by whom, or for whose account, the merchandise was imported knew, or should have known, that the exporter was selling the subject merchandise at less than its fair value,
